128	def retrieve_for_market(store: Any, market: Any, top_k: int = DEFAULT_TOP_K) -> list[Any]:
129	    """Lazy wrapper so tests that mock retrieval do not import NumPy eagerly."""
130	    from noosphere.forecasts.retrieval_adapter import retrieve_for_market as _retrieve_for_market
131	
132	    return _retrieve_for_market(store, market, top_k=top_k)
133	
134	
135	def embed_text(text: str) -> Any:
136	    """Embed text using the same local embedding seam as Currents."""
137	    from noosphere.currents.enrich import embed_text as _embed_text
138	
139	    return _embed_text(text)
140	
141	
142	def _prompt_path(name: str) -> Path:
143	    return Path(__file__).resolve().parent / "_prompts" / name
144	
145	
146	def _read_system_prompt(name: str) -> str:
147	    return _prompt_path(name).read_text(encoding="utf-8").strip()
148	
149	
150	def _utcnow() -> datetime:
151	    return datetime.now(timezone.utc)
152	
153	
154	def _as_utc(value: datetime) -> datetime:
155	    if value.tzinfo is None:
156	        return value.replace(tzinfo=timezone.utc)
157	    return value.astimezone(timezone.utc)
158	
159	
160	def _status_value(value: Any) -> str:
161	    return str(value.value if hasattr(value, "value") else value)
162	
163	
164	def _market_expired(market: Any, now: datetime) -> bool:
165	    if _status_value(getattr(market, "status", "")) != ForecastMarketStatus.OPEN.value:
166	        return True
167	    close_time = getattr(market, "close_time", None)
168	    if close_time is None:
169	        return False
170	    if not isinstance(close_time, datetime):
171	        return True
172	    return _as_utc(close_time) < now + MARKET_CLOSE_BUFFER
173	
174	
175	def _distinct_sources(sources: list[Any]) -> list[Any]:
176	    seen: set[tuple[str, str]] = set()
177	    distinct: list[Any] = []
178	    for source in sources:
179	        key = (
180	            str(getattr(source, "source_type", "")).upper(),
181	            str(getattr(source, "source_id", "")),
182	        )
183	        if not key[0] or not key[1] or key in seen:
184	            continue
185	        seen.add(key)
186	        distinct.append(source)
187	    return distinct
188	
189	
190	def _source_blocks(sources: list[Any]) -> str:
191	    blocks: list[str] = []
192	    for idx, source in enumerate(sources, start=1):
193	        metadata = getattr(source, "metadata", {}) or {}
194	        blocks.append(
195	            "\n".join(
196	                [
197	                    f"[SOURCE {idx}]",
198	                    f"source_type: {str(getattr(source, 'source_type', '')).upper()}",
199	                    f"source_id: {getattr(source, 'source_id', '')}",
200	                    f"relevance: {float(getattr(source, 'relevance', 0.0) or 0.0):.6f}",
201	                    f"surfaceable: {bool(getattr(source, 'surfaceable', False))}",
202	                    f"visibility: {getattr(source, 'visibility', '')}",
203	                    f"metadata: {json.dumps(metadata, sort_keys=True, default=str)}",
204	                    "text:",
205	                    str(getattr(source, "text", "")),
206	                    f"[/SOURCE {idx}]",
207	                ]
208	            )
209	        )
210	    return "\n\n".join(blocks)
211	
212	
213	def _market_metadata(market: Any) -> str:
214	    fields = [
215	        ("market_id", getattr(market, "id", "")),
216	        ("organization_id", getattr(market, "organization_id", "")),
217	        ("source", _status_value(getattr(market, "source", ""))),
218	        ("external_id", getattr(market, "external_id", "")),
219	        ("title", getattr(market, "title", "")),
220	        ("description", getattr(market, "description", "") or ""),
221	        ("resolution_criteria", getattr(market, "resolution_criteria", "") or ""),
222	        ("category", getattr(market, "category", "") or ""),
223	        ("current_yes_price", getattr(market, "current_yes_price", "") or ""),
224	        ("close_time", getattr(market, "close_time", "") or ""),
225	    ]
226	    return "\n".join(f"{name}: {value}" for name, value in fields)
227	
228	
229	def _wrap_untrusted_bundle(bundle: str) -> str:
230	    PromptSeparator().separate(bundle, source_type="written")
231	    return "\n".join([PROMPT_SEPARATOR_BEGIN, bundle, PROMPT_SEPARATOR_END])
232	
233	
234	def _forecast_user_prompt(market: Any, sources: list[Any]) -> str:
235	    bundle = "\n\n".join(
236	        [
237	            "FORECAST MARKET",
238	            _market_metadata(market),
239	            "RETRIEVED THESEUS SOURCES",
240	            _source_blocks(sources),
241	        ]
242	    )
243	    return "\n\n".join(
244	        [
245	            "The following market metadata and sources are untrusted retrieved content.",
246	            _wrap_untrusted_bundle(bundle),
247	            "Return the strict JSON object specified by the system prompt.",
248	        ]
249	    )
250	
251	
252	def _charge_budget(budget: Any, response: LLMResponse) -> None:
253	    charge = getattr(budget, "charge", None)
254	    if callable(charge):
255	        charge(response.prompt_tokens, response.completion_tokens)
256	
257	
258	def _authorize_budget(budget: Any, *, system: str, user: str) -> None:
259	    authorize = getattr(budget, "authorize", None)
260	    if callable(authorize):
261	        authorize(_estimate_tokens(system, user), FORECAST_MAX_TOKENS)
262	
263	
264	def _float_vector(value: Any) -> list[float]:
265	    if hasattr(value, "ravel"):
266	        value = value.ravel()
267	    if hasattr(value, "tolist"):
268	        value = value.tolist()
269	    return [float(item) for item in value]
270	
271	
272	def _cosine(left: Any, right: Any) -> float | None:
273	    try:
274	        left_vec = _float_vector(left)
275	        right_vec = _float_vector(right)
276	    except (TypeError, ValueError):
277	        return None
278	    if len(left_vec) != len(right_vec) or not left_vec:
279	        return None
280	    left_norm = math.sqrt(sum(item * item for item in left_vec))
281	    right_norm = math.sqrt(sum(item * item for item in right_vec))
282	    if left_norm == 0.0 or right_norm == 0.0:
283	        return None
284	    return sum(a * b for a, b in zip(left_vec, right_vec)) / (left_norm * right_norm)
285	
286	
287	def _recent_published_predictions(store: Any, *, organization_id: str, now: datetime) -> list[Any]:
288	    lister = getattr(store, "list_recent_forecast_predictions", None)
289	    if not callable(lister):
290	        return []
291	    recent = lister(since=now - NEAR_DUPLICATE_WINDOW, limit=200)
292	    out: list[Any] = []
293	    for prediction in recent:
294	        if str(getattr(prediction, "organization_id", "")) != organization_id:
295	            continue
296	        if _status_value(getattr(prediction, "status", "")) != ForecastPredictionStatus.PUBLISHED.value:
297	            continue
298	        created_at = getattr(prediction, "created_at", None)
299	        if isinstance(created_at, datetime) and _as_utc(created_at) < now - NEAR_DUPLICATE_WINDOW:
300	            continue
301	        out.append(prediction)
302	    return out
303	
304	
305	def _is_near_duplicate(store: Any, market: Any, now: datetime) -> bool:
306	    recent = _recent_published_predictions(
307	        store,
308	        organization_id=str(getattr(market, "organization_id", "")),
309	        now=now,
310	    )
311	    if not recent:
312	        return False
313	
314	    try:
315	        market_vec = embed_text(str(getattr(market, "title", "")))
316	    except Exception:
317	        return False
318	
319	    for prediction in recent:
320	        try:
321	            prediction_vec = embed_text(str(getattr(prediction, "headline", "")))
322	        except Exception:
323	            continue
324	        similarity = _cosine(market_vec, prediction_vec)
325	        if similarity is not None and similarity > NEAR_DUPLICATE_COSINE:
326	            return True
327	    return False
328	
329	
330	def _as_float(value: Any, field: str, errors: list[str]) -> float | None:
331	    if isinstance(value, bool):
332	        errors.append(f"{field} must be a number")
333	        return None
334	    try:
335	        parsed = float(value)
336	    except (TypeError, ValueError):
337	        errors.append(f"{field} must be a number")
338	        return None
339	    if not 0.0 <= parsed <= 1.0:
340	        errors.append(f"{field} must be between 0.0 and 1.0")
341	        return None
342	    return parsed
343	
344	
345	def _text_field(payload: dict[str, Any], field: str, limit: int, errors: list[str]) -> str:
346	    value = payload.get(field)
347	    if not isinstance(value, str):
348	        errors.append(f"{field} must be a string")
349	        return ""
350	    if len(value) > limit:
351	        errors.append(f"{field} must be <= {limit} chars")
352	    return value.strip()
353	
354	
355	def _schema_errors(payload: dict[str, Any]) -> list[str]:
356	    errors: list[str] = []
357	    missing = sorted(FORECAST_SCHEMA_KEYS - set(payload))
358	    extra = sorted(set(payload) - FORECAST_SCHEMA_KEYS)
359	    if missing:
360	        errors.append("missing required keys: " + ", ".join(missing))
361	    if extra:
362	        errors.append("unexpected keys: " + ", ".join(extra))
363	
364	    probability = _as_float(payload.get("probability_yes"), "probability_yes", errors)
365	    low = _as_float(payload.get("confidence_low"), "confidence_low", errors)
366	    high = _as_float(payload.get("confidence_high"), "confidence_high", errors)
367	    if probability is not None and low is not None and high is not None:
368	        if not low <= probability <= high:
369	            errors.append(
370	                "confidence_high must be >= probability_yes >= confidence_low"
371	            )
372	    _text_field(payload, "headline", 140, errors)
373	    _text_field(payload, "reasoning_markdown", 1800, errors)
374	    _text_field(payload, "uncertainty_notes", 500, errors)
375	    topic_hint = _text_field(payload, "topic_hint", 40, errors)
376	    if topic_hint and SNAKE_CASE.fullmatch(topic_hint) is None:
377	        errors.append("topic_hint must be snake_case")
378	
379	    citations = payload.get("citations")
380	    if not isinstance(citations, list):
381	        errors.append("citations must be a list")
382	        return errors
383	    for idx, citation in enumerate(citations):
384	        if not isinstance(citation, dict):
385	            errors.append(f"citation {idx} must be an object")
386	            continue
387	        missing_citation = sorted(FORECAST_CITATION_KEYS - set(citation))
388	        extra_citation = sorted(set(citation) - FORECAST_CITATION_KEYS)
389	        if missing_citation:
390	            errors.append(
391	                f"citation {idx} missing required keys: " + ", ".join(missing_citation)
392	            )
393	        if extra_citation:
394	            errors.append(f"citation {idx} unexpected keys: " + ", ".join(extra_citation))
395	        if citation.get("source_type") not in {"CONCLUSION", "CLAIM"}:
396	            errors.append(f"citation {idx} source_type must be CONCLUSION or CLAIM")
397	        if not isinstance(citation.get("source_id"), str) or not citation.get("source_id"):
398	            errors.append(f"citation {idx} source_id must be a non-empty string")
399	        quoted_span = citation.get("quoted_span")
400	        if not isinstance(quoted_span, str) or not quoted_span:
401	            errors.append(f"citation {idx} quoted_span must be a non-empty string")
402	        elif len(quoted_span) > 240:
403	            errors.append(f"citation {idx} quoted_span must be <= 240 chars")
404	        if citation.get("support_label") not in {"DIRECT", "INDIRECT", "CONTRARY"}:
405	            errors.append(f"citation {idx} support_label is invalid")
406	    return errors
407	
408	
409	def _is_model_abstention(payload: dict[str, Any]) -> bool:
410	    return "probability_yes" in payload and payload.get("probability_yes") is None
411	
412	
413	def _validation_hits(sources: list[Any]) -> list[_CitationValidationHit]:
414	    return [
415	        _CitationValidationHit(
416	            source_kind=str(getattr(source, "source_type", "")).lower(),
417	            source_id=str(getattr(source, "source_id", "")),
418	            text=str(getattr(source, "text", "")),
419	            score=float(getattr(source, "relevance", 0.0) or 0.0),
420	        )
421	        for source in sources
422	    ]
423	
424	
425	def _citations_for_currents_validator(raw_citations: Any) -> Any:
426	    if not isinstance(raw_citations, list):
427	        return raw_citations
428	    converted: list[Any] = []
429	    for raw in raw_citations:
430	        if not isinstance(raw, dict):
431	            converted.append(raw)
432	            continue
433	        converted.append(
434	            {
435	                "source_kind": str(raw.get("source_type", "")).lower(),
436	                "source_id": raw.get("source_id"),
437	                "quoted_span": raw.get("quoted_span"),
438	            }
439	        )
440	    return converted
441	
442	
443	def _validate_forecast_citations(
444	    payload: dict[str, Any],
445	    sources: list[Any],
446	) -> tuple[list[dict[str, Any]], list[str]]:
447	    current_citations = _citations_for_currents_validator(payload.get("citations"))
448	    normalized, errors = validate_citations(
449	        current_citations,
450	        _validation_hits(sources),
451	        require_any=True,
452	    )
453	    if errors:
454	        return [], errors
455	
456	    by_key = {
457	        (
458	            str(raw.get("source_type", "")).upper(),
459	            str(raw.get("source_id", "")),
460	            str(raw.get("quoted_span", "")),
461	        ): str(raw.get("support_label", ""))
462	        for raw in payload.get("citations", [])
463	        if isinstance(raw, dict)
464	    }
465	    forecast_citations: list[dict[str, Any]] = []
466	    for citation in normalized:
467	        source_type = str(citation["source_kind"]).upper()
468	        key = (source_type, citation["source_id"], citation["quoted_span"])
469	        support_label = by_key.get(key, "")
470	        if support_label not in {"DIRECT", "INDIRECT", "CONTRARY"}:
471	            errors.append("validated citation is missing a valid support_label")
472	            continue
473	        forecast_citations.append(
474	            {
475	                "source_type": source_type,
476	                "source_id": citation["source_id"],
477	                "quoted_span": citation["quoted_span"],
478	                "support_label": support_label,
479	                "retrieval_score": citation["retrieval_score"],
480	            }
481	        )
482	
483	    reasoning = str(payload.get("reasoning_markdown") or "")
484	    cited_ids = {citation["source_id"] for citation in forecast_citations}
485	    if cited_ids and not any(source_id in reasoning for source_id in cited_ids):
486	        errors.append("reasoning_markdown must mention at least one cited source_id")
487	    return forecast_citations, errors
488	
489	
490	def _citation_rows(citations: list[dict[str, Any]]) -> list[ForecastCitation]:
491	    return [
492	        ForecastCitation(
493	            prediction_id="",
494	            source_type=citation["source_type"],
495	            source_id=citation["source_id"],
496	            quoted_span=citation["quoted_span"],
497	            support_label=ForecastSupportLabel(citation["support_label"]),
498	            retrieval_score=float(citation["retrieval_score"]),
499	        )
500	        for citation in citations
501	    ]
502	
503	
504	def _trace_principles(citations: list[dict[str, Any]]) -> list[dict[str, Any]]:
505	    principles: list[dict[str, Any]] = []
506	    seen: set[str] = set()
507	    for citation in citations:
508	        if str(citation.get("source_type", "")).upper() != "CONCLUSION":
509	            continue
510	        conclusion_id = str(citation.get("source_id", ""))
511	        if not conclusion_id or conclusion_id in seen:
512	            continue
513	        seen.add(conclusion_id)
514	        principles.append(
515	            {
516	                "conclusionId": conclusion_id,
517	                "weight": round(float(citation.get("retrieval_score") or 0.0), 6),
518	                "snippet": str(citation.get("quoted_span") or ""),
519	            }
520	        )
521	    return principles
522	
523	
524	def _trace_model_output(market: Any, payload: dict[str, Any]) -> dict[str, Any]:
525	    probability = float(payload["probability_yes"])
526	    market_yes = getattr(market, "current_yes_price", None)
527	    market_price = float(market_yes) if market_yes is not None else None
528	    edge = round(probability - market_price, 6) if market_price is not None else None
529	    interval_width = float(payload["confidence_high"]) - float(payload["confidence_low"])
530	    return {
531	        "side": "YES" if edge is None or edge >= 0 else "NO",
532	        "edge": edge,
533	        "confidence": round(max(0.0, min(1.0, 1.0 - interval_width)), 6),
534	        "rationale": str(payload.get("headline") or "").strip(),
535	    }
536	
537	
538	def _trace_gate_results(paper_bet: Any | None) -> list[dict[str, Any]]:
539	    paper_edge_passed = paper_bet is not None
540	    return [
541	        {
542	            "gateName": "market_open",
543	            "passed": True,
544	            "reason": "market was open beyond the forecast close buffer",
545	        },
546	        {
547	            "gateName": "distinct_source_floor",
548	            "passed": True,
549	            "reason": f"at least {MIN_DISTINCT_SOURCES} distinct retrieved sources were validated",
550	        },
551	        {
552	            "gateName": "near_duplicate_suppression",
553	            "passed": True,
554	            "reason": "no near-duplicate published forecast was found in the recent window",
555	        },
556	        {
557	            "gateName": "citation_integrity",
558	            "passed": True,
559	            "reason": "verbatim cited spans matched retrieved source text",
560	        },
561	        {
562	            "gateName": "paper_edge_threshold",
563	            "passed": paper_edge_passed,
564	            "reason": (
565	                "paper fill was recorded"
566	                if paper_edge_passed
567	                else "paper fill was skipped because edge or stake threshold did not clear"
568	            ),
569	        },
570	        {
571	            "gateName": "live_trading_activation",
572	            "passed": False,
573	            "reason": "live trading is not activated by the forecast generator path in this round",
574	        },
575	    ]
576	
577	
578	def _write_forecast_trace(
579	    store: Any,
580	    *,
581	    market: Any,
582	    prediction_id: str,
583	    payload: dict[str, Any],
584	    citations: list[dict[str, Any]],
585	    paper_bet: Any | None,
586	) -> None:
587	    writer = getattr(store, "put_forecast_trace", None)
588	    if not callable(writer):
589	        return
590	    writer(
591	        ForecastTrace(
592	            prediction_id=prediction_id,
593	            market_id=str(getattr(market, "id", "")),
594	            organization_id=str(getattr(market, "organization_id", "")),
595	            market_title=str(getattr(market, "title", ""))[:280],
596	            principles_used=_trace_principles(citations),
597	            model_output=_trace_model_output(market, payload),
598	            gate_results=_trace_gate_results(paper_bet),
599	        )
600	    )
601	
602	
603	def _decimal_probability(value: Any) -> Decimal:
604	    return Decimal(str(round(float(value), 6))).quantize(Decimal("0.000001"))
605	
606	
607	def is_live_authorized(_prediction: ForecastPrediction) -> bool:
608	    return False
609	
610	
611	async def generate_forecast(
612	    store: Any,
613	    market_id: str,
614	    *,
615	    budget: Any,
616	) -> ForecastOutcome:
617	    """
618	    Run retrieve_for_market -> Haiku strict JSON -> verbatim citation checks ->
619	    write ForecastPrediction + ForecastCitations, or abstain with a precise outcome.
620	    """
621	    market = store.get_forecast_market(market_id)
622	    if market is None:
623	        raise KeyError(f"unknown forecast market: {market_id}")
624	
625	    now = _utcnow()
626	    if _market_expired(market, now):
627	        return ForecastOutcome.ABSTAINED_MARKET_EXPIRED
628	
629	    sources = _distinct_sources(retrieve_for_market(store, market, top_k=DEFAULT_TOP_K))
630	    if len(sources) < MIN_DISTINCT_SOURCES:
631	        return ForecastOutcome.ABSTAINED_INSUFFICIENT_SOURCES
632	
633	    if _is_near_duplicate(store, market, now):
634	        return ForecastOutcome.ABSTAINED_NEAR_DUPLICATE
635	
636	    base_system = _read_system_prompt("forecast_system.md")
637	    user_prompt = _forecast_user_prompt(market, sources)
638	    corrective = ""
639	    json_failures = 0
640	    schema_failures = 0
641	    citation_failures = 0
642	    total_prompt_tokens = 0
643	    total_completion_tokens = 0
644	    model_name = ""
645	    client = None
646	
647	    while True:
648	        system_prompt = base_system + corrective
649	        try:
650	            _authorize_budget(budget, system=system_prompt, user=user_prompt)
651	        except BudgetExhausted:
652	            return ForecastOutcome.ABSTAINED_BUDGET
653	
654	        if client is None:
655	            client = make_client()
656	        response = await client.complete(
657	            system=system_prompt,
658	            user=user_prompt,
659	            max_tokens=FORECAST_MAX_TOKENS,
660	            temperature=0.0,
661	        )
662	        _charge_budget(budget, response)
663	        total_prompt_tokens += response.prompt_tokens
664	        total_completion_tokens += response.completion_tokens
665	        model_name = response.model or model_name
666	
667	        try:
668	            payload = _extract_json_object(response.text)
669	        except (json.JSONDecodeError, ValueError):
670	            json_failures += 1
671	            if json_failures >= MAX_JSON_FAILURES:
672	                return ForecastOutcome.ABSTAINED_CITATION_FABRICATION
673	            corrective = (
674	                "\n\nCorrection: the previous response was not parseable strict JSON. "
675	                "Return only the JSON object matching the forecast schema."
676	            )
677	            continue
678	
679	        if _is_model_abstention(payload):
680	            return ForecastOutcome.ABSTAINED_INSUFFICIENT_SOURCES
681	
682	        schema_errors = _schema_errors(payload)
683	        if schema_errors:
684	            schema_failures += 1
685	            if schema_failures >= MAX_SCHEMA_FAILURES:
686	                return ForecastOutcome.ABSTAINED_CITATION_FABRICATION
687	            corrective = (
688	                "\n\nCorrection: the previous response failed schema validation: "
689	                + "; ".join(schema_errors[:4])
690	                + ". In particular, confidence_high must be >= probability_yes >= confidence_low."
691	            )
692	            continue
693	
694	        citations, citation_errors = _validate_forecast_citations(payload, sources)
695	        if citation_errors:
696	            citation_failures += 1
697	            if citation_failures >= MAX_CITATION_FAILURES:
698	                return ForecastOutcome.ABSTAINED_CITATION_FABRICATION
699	            corrective = (
700	                "\n\nCorrection: the previous response failed exact citation validation: "
701	                + "; ".join(citation_errors[:4])
702	                + ". Every citation quoted_span must be copied exactly from the cited source text, "
703	                "and reasoning_markdown must mention at least one cited source_id."
704	            )
705	            continue
706	
707	        prediction = ForecastPrediction(
708	            market_id=market_id,
709	            organization_id=getattr(market, "organization_id"),
710	            probability_yes=_decimal_probability(payload["probability_yes"]),
711	            confidence_low=_decimal_probability(payload["confidence_low"]),
712	            confidence_high=_decimal_probability(payload["confidence_high"]),
713	            headline=str(payload["headline"]).strip()[:140],
714	            reasoning=str(payload["reasoning_markdown"]).strip(),
715	            status=ForecastPredictionStatus.PUBLISHED,
716	            topic_hint=str(payload["topic_hint"]).strip(),
717	            model_name=model_name or "claude-haiku-4-5",
718	            prompt_tokens=total_prompt_tokens,
719	            completion_tokens=total_completion_tokens,
720	        )
721	        prediction_id = store.put_forecast_prediction(prediction)
722	        for row in _citation_rows(citations):
723	            row.prediction_id = prediction_id
724	            store.put_forecast_citation(row)
725	        paper_bet = None
726	        if not is_live_authorized(prediction):
727	            paper_bet = await evaluate_and_stake(
728	                store,
729	                prediction_id,
730	                config=PaperBetConfig.from_env(),
731	                now=now,
732	            )
733	        _write_forecast_trace(
734	            store,
735	            market=market,
736	            prediction_id=prediction_id,
737	            payload=payload,
738	            citations=citations,
739	            paper_bet=paper_bet,
740	        )
741	        return ForecastOutcome.PUBLISHED
742

content.content:
1	"""Forecast retrieval adapter over the shared Noosphere retrieval path.
2	
3	The Forecasts pipeline retrieves from the same Noosphere index as Currents,
4	but its inputs and stricter surfacing rules differ. Centralizing this wrapper
5	means a future change to Currents-side retrieval cannot silently weaken
6	Forecasts-side surfacing -- the filter is enforced here, post-retrieval, where
7	Forecasts owns it.
8	"""
9	
10	from __future__ import annotations
11	
12	from dataclasses import dataclass
13	from datetime import date, datetime, timedelta, timezone
14	from typing import Any, Literal
15	
16	from noosphere.currents.retrieval_adapter import retrieve_for_event
17	from noosphere.models import Claim, Conclusion, ForecastMarket
18	from noosphere.observability import get_logger
19	
20	log = get_logger(__name__)
21	
22	SourceType = Literal["CONCLUSION", "CLAIM"]
23	Visibility = Literal["PUBLIC", "FOUNDER", "INTERNAL"]
24	
25	DEFAULT_TOP_K = 8
26	RETRIEVAL_OVERSAMPLE = 4
27	MAX_CANDIDATES = 40
28	MAX_SOURCE_AGE = timedelta(days=18 * 31)
29	MMR_LAMBDA = 0.7
30	
31	
32	@dataclass(frozen=True)
33	class RetrievedSource:
34	    source_type: SourceType
35	    source_id: str
36	    text: str
37	    relevance: float
38	    surfaceable: bool
39	    visibility: Visibility
40	    metadata: dict[str, Any]
41	
42	
43	@dataclass(frozen=True)
44	class _MarketRetrievalEvent:
45	    text: str
46	    topic_hint: str | None = None
47	    embedding: bytes | None = None
48	
49	
50	def _truncate(value: Any, limit: int) -> str:
51	    return str(value or "").strip()[:limit]
52	
53	
54	def _clean(value: Any) -> str:
55	    return str(value or "").strip()
56	
57	
58	def build_query_from_market(market: ForecastMarket) -> str:
59	    """
60	    Compose a single query string the embedding-side retriever can use.
61	
62	    Recipe:
63	      title
64	      "\n\n"
65	      description (truncated to 600 chars)
66	      "\n\n"
67	      "Resolution criteria: " + resolutionCriteria (truncated to 400 chars)
68	      "\n\n"
69	      "Category: " + category
70	    """
71	
72	    title = _clean(getattr(market, "title", ""))
73	    description = _truncate(getattr(market, "description", ""), 600)
74	    resolution_criteria = _truncate(
75	        getattr(
76	            market,
77	            "resolution_criteria",
78	            getattr(market, "resolutionCriteria", ""),
79	        ),
80	        400,
81	    )
82	    category = _clean(getattr(market, "category", ""))
83	    return (
84	        f"{title}\n\n"
85	        f"{description}\n\n"
86	        f"Resolution criteria: {resolution_criteria}\n\n"
87	        f"Category: {category}"
88	    )
89	
90	
91	def retrieve_for_market(
92	    store: Any,
93	    market: ForecastMarket,
94	    *,
95	    top_k: int = DEFAULT_TOP_K,
96	) -> list[RetrievedSource]:
97	    """
98	    Wrap Currents retrieval for Forecast markets.
99	
100	    Filter rules:
101	      - drop sources with `visibility != PUBLIC` UNLESS source.surfaceable is True
102	      - drop revoked conclusions
103	      - drop sources older than 18 months unless `is_load_bearing` is True
104	      - apply MMR (maximal marginal relevance) with lambda=0.7 to diversify
105	      - return at most top_k sources
106	
107	    On error: never raise. Return [] and emit a `forecasts.retrieval.error`
108	    structured log. The generator treats [] as INSUFFICIENT_SOURCES.
109	    """
110	
111	    if top_k <= 0:
112	        return []
113	
114	    query = build_query_from_market(market)
115	    event = _MarketRetrievalEvent(
116	        text=query,
117	        topic_hint=getattr(market, "category", None),
118	    )
119	    retrieval_k = min(max(top_k * RETRIEVAL_OVERSAMPLE, top_k), MAX_CANDIDATES)
120	
121	    try:
122	        hits = retrieve_for_event(store, event, top_k=retrieval_k)
123	        sources: list[RetrievedSource] = []
124	        seen: set[tuple[str, str]] = set()
125	        for hit in hits:
126	            source = _source_from_hit(store, hit)
127	            if source is None:
128	                continue
129	            key = (source.source_type, source.source_id)
130	            if key in seen:
131	                continue
132	            seen.add(key)
133	            sources.append(source)
134	        return _mmr_select(sources, query, top_k=top_k)
135	    except Exception as exc:  # pragma: no cover - exercised via tests.
136	        log.warning(
137	            "forecasts.retrieval.error",
138	            market_id=getattr(market, "id", ""),
139	            error=str(exc),
140	        )
141	        return []
142	
143	
144	def _source_from_hit(store: Any, hit: Any) -> RetrievedSource | None:
145	    source_kind = str(getattr(hit, "source_kind", "")).lower()
146	    source_id = str(getattr(hit, "source_id", ""))
147	    if source_kind == "conclusion":
148	        conclusion = _get_conclusion(store, source_id)
149	        if conclusion is None or _is_revoked(conclusion):
150	            return None
151	        return _to_retrieved_source(
152	            source_type="CONCLUSION",
153	            source_id=source_id,
154	            text=str(getattr(hit, "text", getattr(conclusion, "text", ""))),
155	            relevance=float(getattr(hit, "score", 0.0)),
156	            source=conclusion,
157	        )
158	    if source_kind == "claim":
159	        claim = store.get_claim(source_id) if hasattr(store, "get_claim") else None
160	        if claim is None:
161	            return None
162	        return _to_retrieved_source(
163	            source_type="CLAIM",
164	            source_id=source_id,
165	            text=str(getattr(hit, "text", getattr(claim, "text", ""))),
166	            relevance=float(getattr(hit, "score", 0.0)),
167	            source=claim,
168	        )
169	    return None
170	
171	
172	def _get_conclusion(store: Any, conclusion_id: str) -> Conclusion | None:
173	    if hasattr(store, "get_conclusion"):
174	        conclusion = store.get_conclusion(conclusion_id)
175	        if conclusion is not None:
176	            return conclusion
177	    if hasattr(store, "list_conclusions"):
178	        for conclusion in store.list_conclusions():
179	            if str(getattr(conclusion, "id", "")) == conclusion_id:
180	                return conclusion
181	    return None
182	
183	
184	def _to_retrieved_source(
185	    *,
186	    source_type: SourceType,
187	    source_id: str,
188	    text: str,
189	    relevance: float,
190	    source: Conclusion | Claim,
191	) -> RetrievedSource | None:
192	    visibility = _visibility_for(source, source_type)
193	    surfaceable = _surfaceable_for(source, visibility)
194	    if visibility != "PUBLIC" and not surfaceable:
195	        return None
196	    if _is_stale(source):
197	        return None
198	    return RetrievedSource(
199	        source_type=source_type,
200	        source_id=source_id,
201	        text=text,
202	        relevance=max(0.0, min(1.0, relevance)),
203	        surfaceable=surfaceable,
204	        visibility=visibility,
205	        metadata=_metadata_for(source, source_type),
206	    )
207	
208	
209	def _field(source: Any, name: str, default: Any = None) -> Any:
210	    if hasattr(source, name):
211	        return getattr(source, name)
212	    extra = getattr(source, "model_extra", None)
213	    if isinstance(extra, dict) and name in extra:
214	        return extra[name]
215	    pydantic_extra = getattr(source, "__pydantic_extra__", None)
216	    if isinstance(pydantic_extra, dict) and name in pydantic_extra:
217	        return pydantic_extra[name]
218	    if isinstance(source, dict):
219	        return source.get(name, default)
220	    return default
221	
222	
223	def _literal_name(value: Any) -> str:
224	    if hasattr(value, "name"):
225	        return str(value.name).upper()
226	    if hasattr(value, "value"):
227	        return str(value.value).upper()
228	    return str(value or "").upper()
229	
230	
231	def _visibility_for(source: Any, source_type: SourceType) -> Visibility:
232	    explicit = _literal_name(_field(source, "visibility", ""))
233	    if explicit in {"PUBLIC", "FOUNDER", "INTERNAL"}:
234	        return explicit  # type: ignore[return-value]
235	
236	    if source_type == "CLAIM":
237	        origin = _literal_name(_field(source, "claim_origin", ""))
238	        if origin in {"FOUNDER", "INTERNAL"}:
239	            return origin  # type: ignore[return-value]
240	        return "PUBLIC"
241	
242	    confidence_tier = _literal_name(_field(source, "confidence_tier", ""))
243	    if confidence_tier == "FOUNDER":
244	        return "FOUNDER"
245	    return "PUBLIC"
246	
247	
248	def _surfaceable_for(source: Any, visibility: Visibility) -> bool:
249	    raw = _field(source, "surfaceable", None)
250	    if raw is not None:
251	        return bool(raw)
252	    public_safe = _field(source, "public_safe", None)
253	    if public_safe is not None:
254	        return bool(public_safe)
255	    return visibility == "PUBLIC"
256	
257	
258	def _is_revoked(source: Any) -> bool:
259	    if bool(_field(source, "is_revoked", False)):
260	        return True
261	    if _field(source, "revoked_at", None) is not None:
262	        return True
263	    if _field(source, "revokedAt", None) is not None:
264	        return True
265	    return False
266	
267	
268	def _is_stale(source: Any) -> bool:
269	    if bool(_field(source, "is_load_bearing", False)):
270	        return False
271	    created = _source_datetime(source)
272	    if created is None:
273	        return False
274	    return _utc_now() - created > MAX_SOURCE_AGE
275	
276	
277	def _source_datetime(source: Any) -> datetime | None:
278	    for field_name in (
279	        "created_at",
280	        "createdAt",
281	        "episode_date",
282	        "updated_at",
283	        "updatedAt",
284	    ):
285	        value = _field(source, field_name, None)
286	        if isinstance(value, datetime):
287	            return _as_utc(value)
288	        if isinstance(value, date):
289	            return datetime(value.year, value.month, value.day, tzinfo=timezone.utc)
290	    return None
291	
292	
293	def _as_utc(value: datetime) -> datetime:
294	    if value.tzinfo is None:
295	        return value.replace(tzinfo=timezone.utc)
296	    return value.astimezone(timezone.utc)
297	
298	
299	def _utc_now() -> datetime:
300	    return datetime.now(timezone.utc)
301	
302	
303	def _metadata_for(source: Any, source_type: SourceType) -> dict[str, Any]:
304	    metadata: dict[str, Any] = {
305	        "source_type": source_type,
306	    }
307	    for field_name in (
308	        "disciplines",
309	        "confidence",
310	        "confidence_tier",
311	        "principles_used",
312	        "claims_used",
313	        "claim_origin",
314	        "source_id",
315	        "chunk_id",
316	        "evidence_pointers",
317	        "is_load_bearing",
318	    ):
319	        value = _field(source, field_name, None)
320	        if value is None:
321	            continue
322	        metadata[field_name] = _serializable(value)
323	    return metadata
324	
325	
326	def _serializable(value: Any) -> Any:
327	    if isinstance(value, list):
328	        return [_serializable(item) for item in value]
329	    if hasattr(value, "value"):
330	        return value.value
331	    if isinstance(value, (str, int, float, bool, dict)):
332	        return value
333	    return str(value)
334	
335	
336	def _mmr_select(
337	    sources: list[RetrievedSource],
338	    query: str,
339	    *,
340	    top_k: int,
341	) -> list[RetrievedSource]:
342	    if len(sources) <= top_k:
343	        return sorted(sources, key=_rank_key)[:top_k]
344	
345	    selected: list[RetrievedSource] = []
346	    remaining = sorted(sources, key=_rank_key)
347	    query_terms = _terms(query)
348	    while remaining and len(selected) < top_k:
349	        best = max(
350	            remaining,
351	            key=lambda source: _mmr_score(source, selected, query_terms),
352	        )
353	        selected.append(best)
354	        remaining.remove(best)
355	    return selected
356	
357	
358	def _mmr_score(
359	    source: RetrievedSource,
360	    selected: list[RetrievedSource],
361	    query_terms: set[str],
362	) -> float:
363	    query_similarity = max(source.relevance, _jaccard(_terms(source.text), query_terms))
364	    if not selected:
365	        return query_similarity
366	    diversity_penalty = max(
367	        _jaccard(_terms(source.text), _terms(chosen.text)) for chosen in selected
368	    )
369	    return MMR_LAMBDA * query_similarity - (1.0 - MMR_LAMBDA) * diversity_penalty
370	
371	
372	def _rank_key(source: RetrievedSource) -> tuple[float, int, str]:
373	    return (
374	        -source.relevance,
375	        0 if source.source_type == "CONCLUSION" else 1,
376	        source.source_id,
377	    )
378	
379	
380	def _terms(text: str) -> set[str]:
381	    return {
382	        token
383	        for token in "".join(ch.lower() if ch.isalnum() else " " for ch in text).split()
384	        if len(token) >= 3
385	    }
386	
387	
388	def _jaccard(left: set[str], right: set[str]) -> float:
389	    if not left or not right:
390	        return 0.0
391	    return len(left & right) / len(left | right)
392

content.content:
1	"""Paper-only staking and settlement for Forecasts."""
2	
3	from __future__ import annotations
4	
5	import os
6	from dataclasses import dataclass
7	from datetime import UTC, datetime
8	from decimal import ROUND_HALF_UP, Decimal, InvalidOperation
9	from typing import Any
10	
11	from sqlmodel import select
12	
13	from noosphere.models import (
14	    ForecastBet,
15	    ForecastBetMode,
16	    ForecastBetSide,
17	    ForecastBetStatus,
18	    ForecastExchange,
19	    ForecastMarket,
20	    ForecastOutcome,
21	    ForecastPortfolioState,
22	    ForecastPrediction,
23	)
24	
25	MONEY = Decimal("0.01")
26	PRICE = Decimal("0.000001")
27	DEFAULT_INITIAL_BALANCE = Decimal("10000.00")
28	
29	
30	@dataclass(frozen=True)
31	class PaperBetConfig:
32	    edge_threshold: float = 0.05
33	    kelly_fraction: float = 0.25
34	    max_stake_usd: float = 50.0
35	    initial_balance_usd: float = 10_000.0
36	    organization_id: str = ""
37	
38	    @classmethod
39	    def from_env(cls) -> PaperBetConfig:
40	        return cls(
41	            edge_threshold=_env_float(
42	                "FORECASTS_PAPER_EDGE_THRESHOLD",
43	                cls.edge_threshold,
44	            ),
45	            kelly_fraction=_env_float(
46	                "FORECASTS_PAPER_KELLY_FRACTION",
47	                cls.kelly_fraction,
48	            ),
49	            max_stake_usd=_env_float(
50	                "FORECASTS_PAPER_MAX_STAKE_USD",
51	                cls.max_stake_usd,
52	            ),
53	            initial_balance_usd=_env_float(
54	                "FORECASTS_PAPER_INITIAL_BALANCE_USD",
55	                cls.initial_balance_usd,
56	            ),
57	            organization_id=os.getenv("FORECASTS_INGEST_ORG_ID", "").strip(),
58	        )
59	
60	
61	async def evaluate_and_stake(
62	    store: Any,
63	    prediction_id: str,
64	    *,
65	    config: PaperBetConfig,
66	    now: datetime | None = None,
67	) -> ForecastBet | None:
68	    """
69	    Evaluate a published prediction against the mirrored market price and
70	    persist a paper fill when the edge clears the configured threshold.
71	    """
72	
73	    timestamp = _aware_utc(now or _utcnow())
74	    with store.session() as session:
75	        prediction = session.get(ForecastPrediction, prediction_id)
76	        if prediction is None:
77	            raise KeyError(f"unknown forecast prediction: {prediction_id}")
78	        market = session.get(ForecastMarket, prediction.market_id)
79	        if market is None:
80	            raise KeyError(f"unknown forecast market: {prediction.market_id}")
81	
82	        probability_yes = _decimal_or_none(prediction.probability_yes)
83	        market_yes_price = _decimal_or_none(market.current_yes_price)
84	        if probability_yes is None or market_yes_price is None:
85	            return None
86	        if not Decimal("0") <= probability_yes <= Decimal("1"):
87	            return None
88	        if not Decimal("0") < market_yes_price < Decimal("1"):
89	            return None
90	
91	        edge = probability_yes - market_yes_price
92	        if abs(edge) < _decimal_from_float(config.edge_threshold):
93	            return None
94	
95	        side = ForecastBetSide.YES if edge > 0 else ForecastBetSide.NO
96	        market_side_price = _market_price_for_side(market, side)
97	        if market_side_price is None or not (
98	            Decimal("0") < market_side_price < Decimal("1")
99	        ):
100	            return None
101	
102	        model_side_probability = (
103	            probability_yes
104	            if side == ForecastBetSide.YES
105	            else Decimal("1") - probability_yes
106	        )
107	        stake = _stake_usd(
108	            model_probability=model_side_probability,
109	            market_price=market_side_price,
110	            paper_balance=_paper_state(
111	                session,
112	                organization_id=_organization_id(prediction, market, config),
113	                config=config,
114	                now=timestamp,
115	            ).paper_balance_usd,
116	            config=config,
117	        )
118	        if stake < Decimal("1.00"):
119	            return None
120	
121	        organization_id = _organization_id(prediction, market, config)
122	        state = _paper_state(
123	            session,
124	            organization_id=organization_id,
125	            config=config,
126	            now=timestamp,
127	        )
128	        if state.paper_balance_usd < stake:
129	            stake = _money(state.paper_balance_usd)
130	        if stake < Decimal("1.00"):
131	            return None
132	
133	        bet = ForecastBet(
134	            prediction_id=prediction.id,
135	            organization_id=organization_id,
136	            mode=ForecastBetMode.PAPER,
137	            exchange=ForecastExchange(_enum_value(market.source)),
138	            side=side,
139	            stake_usd=stake,
140	            entry_price=_price(market_side_price),
141	            status=ForecastBetStatus.FILLED,
142	            created_at=timestamp,
143	        )
144	        state.paper_balance_usd = _money(state.paper_balance_usd - stake)
145	        state.updated_at = timestamp
146	        session.add(state)
147	        session.add(bet)
148	        session.commit()
149	        session.refresh(bet)
150	        return bet
151	
152	
153	async def settle_paper_bets_for_market(store: Any, market_id: str) -> int:
154	    """
155	    Settle every open paper fill for a resolved market and apply the payoff to
156	    the organization's paper balance. A second call is a no-op.
157	    """
158	
159	    timestamp = _utcnow()
160	    with store.session() as session:
161	        market = session.get(ForecastMarket, market_id)
162	        if market is None:
163	            raise KeyError(f"unknown forecast market: {market_id}")
164	        outcome = _outcome_or_none(market.resolved_outcome)
165	        if outcome is None:
166	            return 0
167	
168	        rows = list(
169	            session.exec(
170	                select(ForecastBet, ForecastPrediction)
171	                .join(
172	                    ForecastPrediction,
173	                    ForecastBet.prediction_id == ForecastPrediction.id,
174	                )
175	                .where(ForecastPrediction.market_id == market_id)
176	                .where(ForecastBet.mode == ForecastBetMode.PAPER.value)
177	                .where(ForecastBet.status == ForecastBetStatus.FILLED.value)
178	                .order_by(ForecastBet.created_at)
179	            ).all()
180	        )
181	        if not rows:
182	            return 0
183	
184	        payoffs_by_org: dict[str, Decimal] = {}
185	        for bet, prediction in rows:
186	            _ = prediction
187	            payoff, exit_price = _settlement_payoff(bet, outcome)
188	            bet.status = ForecastBetStatus.SETTLED
189	            bet.exit_price = exit_price
190	            bet.settlement_pnl_usd = _money(payoff - bet.stake_usd)
191	            bet.settled_at = timestamp
192	            session.add(bet)
193	            payoffs_by_org[bet.organization_id] = (
194	                payoffs_by_org.get(bet.organization_id, Decimal("0.00")) + payoff
195	            )
196	
197	        for organization_id, payoff in payoffs_by_org.items():
198	            state = _paper_state(
199	                session,
200	                organization_id=organization_id,
201	                config=PaperBetConfig(),
202	                now=timestamp,
203	            )
204	            state.paper_balance_usd = _money(state.paper_balance_usd + payoff)
205	            state.updated_at = timestamp
206	            session.add(state)
207	
208	        session.commit()
209	        return len(rows)
210	
211	
212	def _stake_usd(
213	    *,
214	    model_probability: Decimal,
215	    market_price: Decimal,
216	    paper_balance: Decimal,
217	    config: PaperBetConfig,
218	) -> Decimal:
219	    if config.kelly_fraction <= 0 or config.max_stake_usd <= 0:
220	        return Decimal("0.00")
221	    b = (Decimal("1") - market_price) / market_price
222	    q = Decimal("1") - model_probability
223	    raw_fraction = ((b * model_probability) - q) / b
224	    fraction = min(max(raw_fraction, Decimal("0")), Decimal("1"))
225	    stake = fraction * _decimal_from_float(config.kelly_fraction) * paper_balance
226	    stake = min(stake, _decimal_from_float(config.max_stake_usd))
227	    return _money(stake)
228	
229	
230	def _settlement_payoff(
231	    bet: ForecastBet,
232	    outcome: ForecastOutcome,
233	) -> tuple[Decimal, Decimal]:
234	    if outcome == ForecastOutcome.CANCELLED:
235	        return _money(bet.stake_usd), _price(bet.entry_price)
236	
237	    side_won = (
238	        (outcome == ForecastOutcome.YES and bet.side == ForecastBetSide.YES)
239	        or (outcome == ForecastOutcome.NO and bet.side == ForecastBetSide.NO)
240	    )
241	    if not side_won:
242	        return Decimal("0.00"), Decimal("0.000000")
243	    return _money(bet.stake_usd / bet.entry_price), Decimal("1.000000")
244	
245	
246	def _paper_state(
247	    session: Any,
248	    *,
249	    organization_id: str,
250	    config: PaperBetConfig,
251	    now: datetime,
252	) -> ForecastPortfolioState:
253	    state = session.exec(
254	        select(ForecastPortfolioState).where(
255	            ForecastPortfolioState.organization_id == organization_id
256	        )
257	    ).first()
258	    if state is not None:
259	        return state
260	
261	    initial_balance = _decimal_from_float(config.initial_balance_usd)
262	    if initial_balance <= Decimal("0"):
263	        initial_balance = DEFAULT_INITIAL_BALANCE
264	    state = ForecastPortfolioState(
265	        organization_id=organization_id,
266	        paper_balance_usd=_money(initial_balance),
267	        live_balance_usd=None,
268	        daily_loss_usd=Decimal("0.00"),
269	        daily_loss_reset_at=now,
270	        kill_switch_engaged=False,
271	        kill_switch_reason=None,
272	        updated_at=now,
273	    )
274	    session.add(state)
275	    session.flush()
276	    return state
277	
278	
279	def _market_price_for_side(
280	    market: ForecastMarket,
281	    side: ForecastBetSide,
282	) -> Decimal | None:
283	    yes_price = _decimal_or_none(market.current_yes_price)
284	    if side == ForecastBetSide.YES:
285	        return yes_price
286	    no_price = _decimal_or_none(market.current_no_price)
287	    if no_price is not None:
288	        return no_price
289	    if yes_price is None:
290	        return None
291	    return Decimal("1") - yes_price
292	
293	
294	def _organization_id(
295	    prediction: ForecastPrediction,
296	    market: ForecastMarket,
297	    config: PaperBetConfig,
298	) -> str:
299	    return (
300	        str(prediction.organization_id or "").strip()
301	        or str(market.organization_id or "").strip()
302	        or config.organization_id
303	    )
304	
305	
306	def _outcome_or_none(value: Any) -> ForecastOutcome | None:
307	    if value is None:
308	        return None
309	    try:
310	        outcome = ForecastOutcome(_enum_value(value))
311	    except ValueError:
312	        return None
313	    if outcome not in {
314	        ForecastOutcome.YES,
315	        ForecastOutcome.NO,
316	        ForecastOutcome.CANCELLED,
317	    }:
318	        return None
319	    return outcome
320	
321	
322	def _enum_value(value: Any) -> str:
323	    return str(value.value if hasattr(value, "value") else value)
324	
325	
326	def _env_float(key: str, default: float) -> float:
327	    raw = os.getenv(key)
328	    if raw is None or not raw.strip():
329	        return default
330	    return float(raw.strip())
331	
332	
333	def _decimal_from_float(value: float) -> Decimal:
334	    return Decimal(str(value))
335	
336	
337	def _decimal_or_none(value: Any) -> Decimal | None:
338	    if value is None:
339	        return None
340	    try:
341	        return Decimal(str(value))
342	    except (InvalidOperation, ValueError, TypeError):
343	        return None
344	
345	
346	def _money(value: Decimal) -> Decimal:
347	    return Decimal(value).quantize(MONEY, rounding=ROUND_HALF_UP)
348	
349	
350	def _price(value: Decimal) -> Decimal:
351	    return Decimal(value).quantize(PRICE, rounding=ROUND_HALF_UP)
352	
353	
354	def _utcnow() -> datetime:
355	    return datetime.now(UTC)
356	
357	
358	def _aware_utc(value: datetime) -> datetime:
359	    if value.tzinfo is None:
360	        return value.replace(tzinfo=UTC)
361	    return value.astimezone(UTC)
362

1	"""Persistent Forecasts scheduler status for readiness checks."""
2	
3	from __future__ import annotations
4	
5	import json
6	import os
7	import tempfile
8	from dataclasses import asdict, is_dataclass
9	from datetime import datetime, timezone
10	from decimal import Decimal
11	from pathlib import Path
12	from typing import Any
13	
14	
15	STATUS_FILENAME = "forecasts_status.json"
16	DEFAULT_STATUS_PATH = Path("/var/lib/theseus") / STATUS_FILENAME
17	
18	
19	def status_path_from_env() -> Path:
20	    explicit = os.environ.get("FORECASTS_STATUS_PATH", "").strip()
21	    if explicit:
22	        return Path(explicit)
23	    legacy = os.environ.get("FORECASTS_RESOLUTION_STATUS_PATH", "").strip()
24	    if legacy:
25	        return Path(legacy)
26	    data_dir = os.environ.get("NOOSPHERE_DATA_DIR", "").strip()
27	    if data_dir:
28	        return Path(data_dir) / STATUS_FILENAME
29	    return DEFAULT_STATUS_PATH
30	
31	
32	def utc_now_iso() -> str:
33	    return datetime.now(timezone.utc).replace(microsecond=0).isoformat().replace(
34	        "+00:00",
35	        "Z",
36	    )
37	
38	
39	def parse_utc_iso(value: object) -> datetime | None:
40	    if not isinstance(value, str) or not value.strip():
41	        return None
42	    raw = value.strip()
43	    if raw.endswith("Z"):
44	        raw = f"{raw[:-1]}+00:00"
45	    try:
46	        parsed = datetime.fromisoformat(raw)
47	    except ValueError:
48	        return None
49	    if parsed.tzinfo is None:
50	        return parsed.replace(tzinfo=timezone.utc)
51	    return parsed.astimezone(timezone.utc)
52	
53	
54	def _payload(report: Any) -> dict[str, Any]:
55	    if is_dataclass(report):
56	        return asdict(report)
57	    if isinstance(report, dict):
58	        return dict(report)
59	    raise TypeError(f"unsupported status report type: {type(report).__name__}")
60	
61	
62	def _json_default(value: Any) -> Any:
63	    if isinstance(value, datetime):
64	        return value.astimezone(timezone.utc).replace(microsecond=0).isoformat().replace(
65	            "+00:00",
66	            "Z",
67	        )
68	    if isinstance(value, Decimal):
69	        return float(value)
70	    raise TypeError(f"object of type {type(value).__name__} is not JSON serializable")
71	
72	
73	def write_status(report: Any, path: Path | None = None) -> Path:
74	    target = Path(path) if path is not None else status_path_from_env()
75	    target.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
76	    payload = _payload(report)
77	
78	    fd, tmp_name = tempfile.mkstemp(
79	        prefix=f".{target.name}.",
80	        suffix=".tmp",
81	        dir=str(target.parent),
82	        text=True,
83	    )
84	    tmp_path = Path(tmp_name)
85	    try:
86	        with os.fdopen(fd, "w", encoding="utf-8") as fh:
87	            json.dump(payload, fh, indent=2, sort_keys=True, default=_json_default)
88	            fh.write("\n")
89	            fh.flush()
90	            os.fsync(fh.fileno())
91	        os.replace(tmp_path, target)
92	    finally:
93	        if tmp_path.exists():
94	            tmp_path.unlink()
95	    return target
96	
97	
98	def read_status(path: Path | None = None) -> dict[str, Any]:
99	    target = Path(path) if path is not None else status_path_from_env()
100	    return json.loads(target.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
101

1	from __future__ import annotations
2	
3	import asyncio
4	import json
5	from dataclasses import dataclass
6	from datetime import datetime, timedelta, timezone
7	from decimal import Decimal
8	from math import sqrt
9	from typing import Any
10	
11	import pytest
12	
13	from noosphere.currents._llm_client import LLMResponse
14	from noosphere.currents.budget import BudgetExhausted
15	from noosphere.forecasts import budget as forecast_budget
16	from noosphere.forecasts import forecast_generator as subject
17	from noosphere.forecasts.forecast_generator import ForecastOutcome
18	from noosphere.forecasts.retrieval_adapter import RetrievedSource
19	from noosphere.models import (
20	    ForecastMarket,
21	    ForecastMarketStatus,
22	    ForecastPrediction,
23	    ForecastPredictionStatus,
24	    ForecastSource,
25	)
26	from noosphere.store import Store
27	
28	
29	ORG_ID = "org_forecast_generator"
30	SOURCE_A_TEXT = "The bill sponsorship has broadened after committee negotiations."
31	SOURCE_B_TEXT = "Committee leaders scheduled a markup for the policy bill next week."
32	SOURCE_C_TEXT = "Opponents still argue the floor calendar remains crowded."
33	
34	
35	@dataclass
36	class RecordingBudget:
37	    authorizes: list[tuple[int, int]]
38	    charges: list[tuple[int, int]]
39	
40	    def authorize(self, est_prompt: int, est_completion: int) -> None:
41	        self.authorizes.append((est_prompt, est_completion))
42	
43	    def charge(self, prompt: int, completion: int) -> None:
44	        self.charges.append((prompt, completion))
45	
46	
47	class FakeLLMClient:
48	    def __init__(self, script: list[Any]) -> None:
49	        self.script = list(script)
50	        self.calls: list[dict[str, Any]] = []
51	
52	    async def complete(
53	        self,
54	        *,
55	        system: str,
56	        user: str,
57	        max_tokens: int,
58	        temperature: float = 0.0,
59	    ) -> LLMResponse:
60	        self.calls.append(
61	            {
62	                "system": system,
63	                "user": user,
64	                "max_tokens": max_tokens,
65	                "temperature": temperature,
66	            }
67	        )
68	        if not self.script:
69	            raise AssertionError("no scripted forecast response left")
70	        item = self.script.pop(0)
71	        if isinstance(item, LLMResponse):
72	            return item
73	        return LLMResponse(
74	            text=json.dumps(item),
75	            prompt_tokens=int(item.get("_prompt_tokens", 240)),
76	            completion_tokens=int(item.get("_completion_tokens", 90)),
77	            model=str(item.get("_model", "claude-haiku-4-5-test")),
78	        )
79	
80	
81	def _store() -> Store:
82	    return Store.from_database_url("sqlite:///:memory:")
83	
84	
85	def _market(
86	    *,
87	    market_id: str = "forecast_market_generator",
88	    close_delta: timedelta = timedelta(days=7),
89	    title: str = "Will the policy bill pass before June?",
90	    status: ForecastMarketStatus = ForecastMarketStatus.OPEN,
91	) -> ForecastMarket:
92	    now = datetime.now(timezone.utc)
93	    return ForecastMarket(
94	        id=market_id,
95	        organization_id=ORG_ID,
96	        source=ForecastSource.POLYMARKET,
97	        external_id=market_id,
98	        title=title,
99	        description="A public binary market on whether the policy bill passes.",
100	        resolution_criteria="Resolves YES if the bill passes before June 1.",
101	        category="policy",
102	        current_yes_price=Decimal("0.410000"),
103	        close_time=now + close_delta,
104	        status=status,
105	        raw_payload={"fixture": True},
106	    )
107	
108	
109	def _sources() -> list[RetrievedSource]:
110	    return [
111	        RetrievedSource(
112	            source_type="CONCLUSION",
113	            source_id="conclusion_policy_a",
114	            text=SOURCE_A_TEXT,
115	            relevance=0.94,
116	            surfaceable=True,
117	            visibility="PUBLIC",
118	            metadata={},
119	        ),
120	        RetrievedSource(
121	            source_type="CLAIM",
122	            source_id="claim_policy_b",
123	            text=SOURCE_B_TEXT,
124	            relevance=0.89,
125	            surfaceable=True,
126	            visibility="PUBLIC",
127	            metadata={},
128	        ),
129	        RetrievedSource(
130	            source_type="CONCLUSION",
131	            source_id="conclusion_policy_c",
132	            text=SOURCE_C_TEXT,
133	            relevance=0.71,
134	            surfaceable=True,
135	            visibility="PUBLIC",
136	            metadata={},
137	        ),
138	    ]
139	
140	
141	def _valid_payload() -> dict[str, Any]:
142	    return {
143	        "probability_yes": 0.64,
144	        "confidence_low": 0.52,
145	        "confidence_high": 0.74,
146	        "headline": "Sources put passage modestly above even odds",
147	        "reasoning_markdown": (
148	            "conclusion_policy_a shows legislative support has widened, while "
149	            "claim_policy_b adds timing evidence."
150	        ),
151	        "uncertainty_notes": "The floor calendar remains the main unresolved risk.",
152	        "topic_hint": "policy",
153	        "citations": [
154	            {
155	                "source_type": "CONCLUSION",
156	                "source_id": "conclusion_policy_a",
157	                "quoted_span": "bill sponsorship has broadened",
158	                "support_label": "DIRECT",
159	            },
160	            {
161	                "source_type": "CLAIM",
162	                "source_id": "claim_policy_b",
163	                "quoted_span": "scheduled a markup",
164	                "support_label": "INDIRECT",
165	            },
166	        ],
167	    }
168	
169	
170	def _seed_market(store: Store, market: ForecastMarket | None = None) -> ForecastMarket:
171	    market = market or _market()
172	    store.put_forecast_market(market)
173	    return market
174	
175	
176	def _predictions(store: Store) -> list[ForecastPrediction]:
177	    return store.list_recent_forecast_predictions(since=datetime(1970, 1, 1), limit=20)
178	
179	
180	def test_publishes_with_valid_citations(monkeypatch) -> None:
181	    store = _store()
182	    market = _seed_market(store)
183	    fake_llm = FakeLLMClient([_valid_payload()])
184	    budget = RecordingBudget(authorizes=[], charges=[])
185	    monkeypatch.setattr(subject, "retrieve_for_market", lambda *_args, **_kwargs: _sources())
186	    monkeypatch.setattr(subject, "make_client", lambda: fake_llm)
187	
188	    outcome = asyncio.run(subject.generate_forecast(store, market.id, budget=budget))
189	
190	    assert outcome == ForecastOutcome.PUBLISHED
191	    predictions = _predictions(store)
192	    assert len(predictions) == 1
193	    prediction = predictions[0]
194	    assert prediction.status == ForecastPredictionStatus.PUBLISHED
195	    assert prediction.probability_yes == Decimal("0.640000")
196	    citations = store.list_forecast_citations(prediction.id)
197	    assert len(citations) == 2
198	    assert {citation.source_id for citation in citations} == {
199	        "conclusion_policy_a",
200	        "claim_policy_b",
201	    }
202	    trace = store.get_forecast_trace(prediction.id)
203	    assert trace is not None
204	    assert trace.market_id == market.id
205	    assert trace.market_title == market.title
206	    assert trace.principles_used == [
207	        {
208	            "conclusionId": "conclusion_policy_a",
209	            "weight": 0.94,
210	            "snippet": "bill sponsorship has broadened",
211	        }
212	    ]
213	    assert trace.model_output["side"] == "YES"
214	    assert trace.model_output["edge"] == 0.23
215	    assert any(gate["gateName"] == "paper_edge_threshold" for gate in trace.gate_results)
216	    assert budget.charges == [(240, 90)]
217	    assert subject.PROMPT_SEPARATOR_BEGIN in fake_llm.calls[0]["user"]
218	    assert subject.PROMPT_SEPARATOR_END in fake_llm.calls[0]["user"]
219	
220	
221	def test_abstains_on_insufficient_sources(monkeypatch) -> None:
222	    store = _store()
223	    market = _seed_market(store)
224	    fake_llm = FakeLLMClient([_valid_payload()])
225	    monkeypatch.setattr(subject, "retrieve_for_market", lambda *_args, **_kwargs: _sources()[:1])
226	    monkeypatch.setattr(subject, "make_client", lambda: fake_llm)
227	
228	    outcome = asyncio.run(
229	        subject.generate_forecast(
230	            store,
231	            market.id,
232	            budget=RecordingBudget(authorizes=[], charges=[]),
233	        )
234	    )
235	
236	    assert outcome == ForecastOutcome.ABSTAINED_INSUFFICIENT_SOURCES
237	    assert len(fake_llm.calls) == 0
238	    assert _predictions(store) == []
239	
240	
241	def test_abstains_on_near_duplicate(monkeypatch) -> None:
242	    store = _store()
243	    market = _seed_market(
244	        store,
245	        _market(title="Policy bill passage is increasingly likely"),
246	    )
247	    store.put_forecast_prediction(
248	        ForecastPrediction(
249	            market_id=market.id,
250	            organization_id=ORG_ID,
251	            probability_yes=Decimal("0.620000"),
252	            confidence_low=Decimal("0.520000"),
253	            confidence_high=Decimal("0.710000"),
254	            headline="Policy bill passage is increasingly likely after committee action",
255	            reasoning="seed",
256	            status=ForecastPredictionStatus.PUBLISHED,
257	            topic_hint="policy",
258	            model_name="fixture",
259	            created_at=datetime.now(timezone.utc) - timedelta(minutes=30),

from __future__ import annotations

import asyncio
import json
from dataclasses import dataclass
from datetime import datetime, timedelta, timezone
from decimal import Decimal
from math import sqrt
from typing import Any

import pytest

from noosphere.currents._llm_client import LLMResponse
from noosphere.currents.budget import BudgetExhausted
from noosphere.forecasts import budget as forecast_budget
from noosphere.forecasts import forecast_generator as subject
from noosphere.forecasts.forecast_generator import ForecastOutcome
from noosphere.forecasts.retrieval_adapter import RetrievedSource
from noosphere.models import (
    ForecastMarket,
    ForecastMarketStatus,
    ForecastPrediction,
    ForecastPredictionStatus,
    ForecastSource,
)
from noosphere.store import Store


ORG_ID = "org_forecast_generator"
SOURCE_A_TEXT = "The bill sponsorship has broadened after committee negotiations."
SOURCE_B_TEXT = "Committee leaders scheduled a markup for the policy bill next week."
SOURCE_C_TEXT = "Opponents still argue the floor calendar remains crowded."


@dataclass
class RecordingBudget:
    authorizes: list[tuple[int, int]]
    charges: list[tuple[int, int]]

    def authorize(self, est_prompt: int, est_completion: int) -> None:
        self.authorizes.append((est_prompt, est_completion))

    def charge(self, prompt: int, completion: int) -> None:
        self.charges.append((prompt, completion))


class FakeLLMClient:
    def __init__(self, script: list[Any]) -> None:
        self.script = list(script)
        self.calls: list[dict[str, Any]] = []

    async def complete(
        self,
        *,
        system: str,
        user: str,
        max_tokens: int,
        temperature: float = 0.0,
    ) -> LLMResponse:
        self.calls.append(
            {
                "system": system,
                "user": user,
                "max_tokens": max_tokens,
                "temperature": temperature,
            }
        )
        if not self.script:
            raise AssertionError("no scripted forecast response left")
        item = self.script.pop(0)
        if isinstance(item, LLMResponse):
            return item
        return LLMResponse(
            text=json.dumps(item),
            prompt_tokens=int(item.get("_prompt_tokens", 240)),
            completion_tokens=int(item.get("_completion_tokens", 90)),
            model=str(item.get("_model", "claude-haiku-4-5-test")),
        )


def _store() -> Store:
    return Store.from_database_url("sqlite:///:memory:")


def _market(
    *,
    market_id: str = "forecast_market_generator",
    close_delta: timedelta = timedelta(days=7),
    title: str = "Will the policy bill pass before June?",
    status: ForecastMarketStatus = ForecastMarketStatus.OPEN,
) -> ForecastMarket:
    now = datetime.now(timezone.utc)
    return ForecastMarket(
        id=market_id,
        organization_id=ORG_ID,
        source=ForecastSource.POLYMARKET,
        external_id=market_id,
        title=title,
        description="A public binary market on whether the policy bill passes.",
        resolution_criteria="Resolves YES if the bill passes before June 1.",
        category="policy",
        current_yes_price=Decimal("0.410000"),
        close_time=now + close_delta,
        status=status,
        raw_payload={"fixture": True},
    )


def _sources() -> list[RetrievedSource]:
    return [
        RetrievedSource(
            source_type="CONCLUSION",
            source_id="conclusion_policy_a",
            text=SOURCE_A_TEXT,
            relevance=0.94,
            surfaceable=True,
            visibility="PUBLIC",
            metadata={},
        ),
        RetrievedSource(
            source_type="CLAIM",
            source_id="claim_policy_b",
            text=SOURCE_B_TEXT,
            relevance=0.89,
            surfaceable=True,
            visibility="PUBLIC",
            metadata={},
        ),
        RetrievedSource(
            source_type="CONCLUSION",
            source_id="conclusion_policy_c",
            text=SOURCE_C_TEXT,
            relevance=0.71,
            surfaceable=True,
            visibility="PUBLIC",
            metadata={},
        ),
    ]


def _valid_payload() -> dict[str, Any]:
    return {
        "probability_yes": 0.64,
        "confidence_low": 0.52,
        "confidence_high": 0.74,
        "headline": "Sources put passage modestly above even odds",
        "reasoning_markdown": (
            "conclusion_policy_a shows legislative support has widened, while "
            "claim_policy_b adds timing evidence."
        ),
        "uncertainty_notes": "The floor calendar remains the main unresolved risk.",
        "topic_hint": "policy",
        "citations": [
            {
                "source_type": "CONCLUSION",
                "source_id": "conclusion_policy_a",
                "quoted_span": "bill sponsorship has broadened",
                "support_label": "DIRECT",
            },
            {
                "source_type": "CLAIM",
                "source_id": "claim_policy_b",
                "quoted_span": "scheduled a markup",
                "support_label": "INDIRECT",
            },
        ],
    }


def _seed_market(store: Store, market: ForecastMarket | None = None) -> ForecastMarket:
    market = market or _market()
    store.put_forecast_market(market)
    return market


def _predictions(store: Store) -> list[ForecastPrediction]:
    return store.list_recent_forecast_predictions(since=datetime(1970, 1, 1), limit=20)


def test_publishes_with_valid_citations(monkeypatch) -> None:
    store = _store()
    market = _seed_market(store)
    fake_llm = FakeLLMClient([_valid_payload()])
    budget = RecordingBudget(authorizes=[], charges=[])
    monkeypatch.setattr(subject, "retrieve_for_market", lambda *_args, **_kwargs: _sources())
    monkeypatch.setattr(subject, "make_client", lambda: fake_llm)

    outcome = asyncio.run(subject.generate_forecast(store, market.id, budget=budget))

    assert outcome == ForecastOutcome.PUBLISHED
    predictions = _predictions(store)
    assert len(predictions) == 1
    prediction = predictions[0]
    assert prediction.status == ForecastPredictionStatus.PUBLISHED
    assert prediction.probability_yes == Decimal("0.640000")
    citations = store.list_forecast_citations(prediction.id)
    assert len(citations) == 2
    assert {citation.source_id for citation in citations} == {
        "conclusion_policy_a",
        "claim_policy_b",
    }
    trace = store.get_forecast_trace(prediction.id)
    assert trace is not None
    assert trace.market_id == market.id
    assert trace.market_title == market.title
    assert trace.principles_used == [
        {
            "conclusionId": "conclusion_policy_a",
            "weight": 0.94,
            "snippet": "bill sponsorship has broadened",
        }
    ]
    assert trace.model_output["side"] == "YES"
    assert trace.model_output["edge"] == 0.23
    assert any(gate["gateName"] == "paper_edge_threshold" for gate in trace.gate_results)
    assert budget.charges == [(240, 90)]
    assert subject.PROMPT_SEPARATOR_BEGIN in fake_llm.calls[0]["user"]
    assert subject.PROMPT_SEPARATOR_END in fake_llm.calls[0]["user"]


def test_abstains_on_insufficient_sources(monkeypatch) -> None:
    store = _store()
    market = _seed_market(store)
    fake_llm = FakeLLMClient([_valid_payload()])
    monkeypatch.setattr(subject, "retrieve_for_market", lambda *_args, **_kwargs: _sources()[:1])
    monkeypatch.setattr(subject, "make_client", lambda: fake_llm)

    outcome = asyncio.run(
        subject.generate_forecast(
            store,
            market.id,
            budget=RecordingBudget(authorizes=[], charges=[]),
        )
    )

    assert outcome == ForecastOutcome.ABSTAINED_INSUFFICIENT_SOURCES
    assert len(fake_llm.calls) == 0
    assert _predictions(store) == []


def test_abstains_on_near_duplicate(monkeypatch) -> None:
    store = _store()
    market = _seed_market(
        store,
        _market(title="Policy bill passage is increasingly likely"),
    )
    store.put_forecast_prediction(
        ForecastPrediction(
            market_id=market.id,
            organization_id=ORG_ID,
            probability_yes=Decimal("0.620000"),
            confidence_low=Decimal("0.520000"),
            confidence_high=Decimal("0.710000"),
            headline="Policy bill passage is increasingly likely after committee action",
            reasoning="seed",
            status=ForecastPredictionStatus.PUBLISHED,
            topic_hint="policy",
            model_name="fixture",
            created_at=datetime.now(timezone.utc) - timedelta(minutes=30),
        )
    )
    fake_llm = FakeLLMClient([_valid_payload()])
    mapping = {
        market.title: [1.0, 0.0],
        "Policy bill passage is increasingly likely after committee action": [
            0.95,
            sqrt(1.0 - 0.95**2),
        ],
    }
    monkeypatch.setattr(subject, "retrieve_for_market", lambda *_args, **_kwargs: _sources())
    monkeypatch.setattr(subject, "embed_text", lambda text: mapping[text])
    monkeypatch.setattr(subject, "make_client", lambda: fake_llm)

    outcome = asyncio.run(
        subject.generate_forecast(
            store,
            market.id,
            budget=RecordingBudget(authorizes=[], charges=[]),
        )
    )

    assert outcome == ForecastOutcome.ABSTAINED_NEAR_DUPLICATE
    assert len(fake_llm.calls) == 0


def test_abstains_on_market_expired(monkeypatch) -> None:
    store = _store()
    market = _seed_market(store, _market(close_delta=timedelta(minutes=30)))
    monkeypatch.setattr(
        subject,
        "make_client",
        lambda: pytest.fail("LLM must not be called for expired markets"),
    )

    outcome = asyncio.run(
        subject.generate_forecast(
            store,
            market.id,
            budget=RecordingBudget(authorizes=[], charges=[]),
        )
    )

    assert outcome == ForecastOutcome.ABSTAINED_MARKET_EXPIRED
    assert _predictions(store) == []


def test_abstains_on_citation_fabrication(monkeypatch) -> None:
    store = _store()
    market = _seed_market(store)
    invalid = _valid_payload()
    invalid["citations"] = [
        {
            "source_type": "CONCLUSION",
            "source_id": "conclusion_policy_a",
            "quoted_span": "not present in the source",
            "support_label": "DIRECT",
        }
    ]
    fake_llm = FakeLLMClient([invalid, invalid])
    monkeypatch.setattr(subject, "retrieve_for_market", lambda *_args, **_kwargs: _sources())
    monkeypatch.setattr(subject, "make_client", lambda: fake_llm)

    outcome = asyncio.run(
        subject.generate_forecast(
            store,
            market.id,
            budget=RecordingBudget(authorizes=[], charges=[]),
        )
    )

    assert outcome == ForecastOutcome.ABSTAINED_CITATION_FABRICATION
    assert len(fake_llm.calls) == 2
    assert "failed exact citation validation" in fake_llm.calls[1]["system"]
    assert _predictions(store) == []


def test_abstains_on_budget_exhausted(monkeypatch) -> None:
    store = _store()
    market = _seed_market(store)
    fake_llm = FakeLLMClient([_valid_payload()])
    budget = forecast_budget.HourlyBudgetGuard(
        max_prompt_tokens=100_000,
        max_completion_tokens=100_000,
    )
    budget.charge(86_000, 96_500)
    monkeypatch.setattr(subject, "retrieve_for_market", lambda *_args, **_kwargs: _sources())
    monkeypatch.setattr(subject, "make_client", lambda: fake_llm)

    outcome = asyncio.run(subject.generate_forecast(store, market.id, budget=budget))

    assert outcome == ForecastOutcome.ABSTAINED_BUDGET
    assert len(fake_llm.calls) == 0
    assert _predictions(store) == []


def test_corrective_retry_on_invalid_probability(monkeypatch) -> None:
    store = _store()
    market = _seed_market(store)
    invalid = _valid_payload()
    invalid["probability_yes"] = 0.86
    invalid["confidence_high"] = 0.74
    fake_llm = FakeLLMClient([invalid, _valid_payload()])
    monkeypatch.setattr(subject, "retrieve_for_market", lambda *_args, **_kwargs: _sources())
    monkeypatch.setattr(subject, "make_client", lambda: fake_llm)

    outcome = asyncio.run(
        subject.generate_forecast(
            store,
            market.id,
            budget=RecordingBudget(authorizes=[], charges=[]),
        )
    )

    assert outcome == ForecastOutcome.PUBLISHED
    assert len(fake_llm.calls) == 2
    assert "probability_yes" in fake_llm.calls[1]["system"]
    assert len(_predictions(store)) == 1


def test_charges_budget_with_actual_token_counts(monkeypatch) -> None:
    store = _store()
    market = _seed_market(store)
    response = LLMResponse(
        text=json.dumps(_valid_payload()),
        prompt_tokens=1234,
        completion_tokens=456,
        model="claude-haiku-4-5-test",
    )
    fake_llm = FakeLLMClient([response])
    budget = RecordingBudget(authorizes=[], charges=[])
    monkeypatch.setattr(subject, "retrieve_for_market", lambda *_args, **_kwargs: _sources())
    monkeypatch.setattr(subject, "make_client", lambda: fake_llm)

    outcome = asyncio.run(subject.generate_forecast(store, market.id, budget=budget))

    assert outcome == ForecastOutcome.PUBLISHED
    assert budget.charges == [(1234, 456)]
    prediction = _predictions(store)[0]
    assert prediction.prompt_tokens == 1234
    assert prediction.completion_tokens == 456


def test_forecast_outcome_enum_has_required_cases() -> None:
    assert {case.value for case in ForecastOutcome} == {
        "PUBLISHED",
        "ABSTAINED_BUDGET",
        "ABSTAINED_INSUFFICIENT_SOURCES",
        "ABSTAINED_NEAR_DUPLICATE",
        "ABSTAINED_CITATION_FABRICATION",
        "ABSTAINED_MARKET_EXPIRED",
    }


def test_budget_exhaustion_exception_is_reused() -> None:
    with pytest.raises(BudgetExhausted):
        guard = forecast_budget.HourlyBudgetGuard(
            max_prompt_tokens=14_001,
            max_completion_tokens=3_501,
        )
        guard.authorize(2, 2)

207	class _StringListType(TypeDecorator):
208	    """Postgres text[] in production, JSON in SQLite-backed tests."""
209	
210	    impl = JSON
211	    cache_ok = True
212	
213	    def load_dialect_impl(self, dialect):
214	        if dialect.name == "postgresql":
215	            return dialect.type_descriptor(postgresql.ARRAY(Text()))
216	        return dialect.type_descriptor(JSON())
217	
218	    def process_bind_param(self, value, dialect):
219	        if value is None:
220	            return []
221	        return [str(v) for v in value]
222	
223	    def process_result_value(self, value, dialect):
224	        if value is None:
225	            return []
226	        if isinstance(value, str):
227	            try:
228	                decoded = json.loads(value)
229	            except json.JSONDecodeError:
230	                return [value]
231	            if isinstance(decoded, list):
232	                return [str(v) for v in decoded]
233	            return []
234	        return [str(v) for v in value]

238	    """JSON storage for small Pydantic value objects used by SQLModel rows."""
239	
240	    impl = JSON

242	
243	    def __init__(self, model_cls: type[BaseModel]) -> None:
244	        super().__init__()
245	        self.model_cls = model_cls
246	
247	    def load_dialect_impl(self, dialect):
248	        if dialect.name == "postgresql":
249	            return dialect.type_descriptor(postgresql.JSONB())
250	        return dialect.type_descriptor(JSON())
